differentials.name: FUNCTIONAL ASPLENIA =  marked decrease in splenic phagocytic function despite presence of splenic tissue within the body
differentials.raw: FUNCTIONAL ASPLENIA =  marked decrease in splenic phagocytic function despite presence of splenic tissue within the body

differentials.name: Circulatory disturbances: occlusion of splenic artery / vein, hemoglobinopathies (sickle cell disease, hemoglobin-SC disease, thalassemia), polycythemia vera, idiopathic thrombocytopenic purpura
differentials.raw: Circulatory disturbances: occlusion of splenic artery / vein, hemoglobinopathies (sickle cell disease, hemoglobin-SC disease, thalassemia), polycythemia vera, idiopathic thrombocytopenic purpura

differentials.name: Altered RES activity: thorotrast, irradiation, combined splenic irradiation + chemotherapy, replacement of RES by tumor / infiltrate, splenic anoxia (cyanotic congenital heart disease), sprue
differentials.raw: Altered RES activity: thorotrast, irradiation, combined splenic irradiation + chemotherapy, replacement of RES by tumor / infiltrate, splenic anoxia (cyanotic congenital heart disease), sprue

differentials.name: Autoimmune disease Cx:   children at risk for pneumococcal pneumonia
differentials.raw: Autoimmune disease Cx:   children at risk for pneumococcal pneumonia (liver partially takes over immune response later in life)

differentials.name: FUNCTIONAL ASPLENIA + SPLENIC ATROPHY ulcerative colitis, Crohn disease, celiac disease, tropical sprue, dermatitis herpetiformis, thyrotoxicosis, idiopathic thrombocytopenic purpura, thorotrast
differentials.raw: FUNCTIONAL ASPLENIA + SPLENIC ATROPHY ulcerative colitis, Crohn disease, celiac disease, tropical sprue, dermatitis herpetiformis, thyrotoxicosis, idiopathic thrombocytopenic purpura, thorotrast

differentials.name: FUNCTIONAL ASPLENIA + NORMAL / LARGE SPLEEN sarcoidosis, amyloidosis, sickle cell anemia (if not infarcted), after bone marrow transplantation
differentials.raw: FUNCTIONAL ASPLENIA + NORMAL / LARGE SPLEEN sarcoidosis, amyloidosis, sickle cell anemia (if not infarcted), after bone marrow transplantation

context: Hyposplenism =  no uptake of 99mTc-sulfur colloid √  spleen not visualized on 99mTc-sulfur colloid √  99mTc-heat-damaged RBCs / 111In-labeled platelets may demonstrate splenic tissue if 99mTc-sulfur colloid does not Cx:     increased risk of infection (pneumococcus, meningococcus, influenza) ANATOMY OF LIVER, BILE DUCTS, PANCREAS AND SPLEEN
